figures.caption: Table 2: Topics with their interpretive label, per-topic dominant-document count nk, and mean prevalence γ̄k.

Figure 1: Top 20 terms per topic (prevalence-weighted). Panel area per topic is proportional to its mean prevalence; tile area per lemma is proportional to its within-topic weight. Italian lemmas (left); English glosses (right).
Table 5: Evgenij’s reply — lexical hub card.
Figure 4: Chord diagram showing interrelations between the five main themes (bottom) and their associated subthemes (top).
Table 4: Tat’jana’s letter — lexical hub card. sPLS–DA lemmas are denoted by † or ‡; †, novel lemma (not in LDA top lists); ‡, porous lemma (appearing among the top terms of another topic).
Figure 3: Cross-over heatmap between sPLS–DA core-exclusive lemmas and LDA top lemmas. Diagonal values indicate within-theme overlap; off-diagonal values indicate cross-theme overlap.
Table 1: Selected list of narrative hubs in Evgenij Onegin, with corresponding chapter (ch.) and stanza ranges.
Table 3: Cross-validated performance of sPLS–DA OvR classifiers by topic. Columns: mean accuracy (acc), balanced accuracy (bacc), and stratified random baselines (acc0, bacc0).
Figure 2: Narrative map across documents. Grey bands mark the narrative hubs described in Table 1.
